title: Viburnum stenocalyx
intro: Viburnum stenocalyx, also known as the mountain sweetberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Viburnum stenocalyx include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Viburnum stenocalyx is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Viburnum stenocalyx is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the elderberry or moschatel family. This plant is native to the southwestern region of China. The Adoxaceae family consists of around 200 species of plants, primarily shrubs and trees. The classification of Viburnum stenocalyx is further divided into several subcategories, including the genus Viburnum, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia and Europe. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Viburnum stenocalyx is a plant species native to China and India.
